title: Serapias × dupuyana
taxonomy: Serapias × dupuyana is a nothogenus, resulting from a natural hybridization of two different species of the genus Serapias. This classification is used to denote the unique characteristics and genetic makeup of the resulting plant. The nothogenus is often used to distinguish hybrids from their parent species. The classification helps scientists understand the genetic relationships between different plant species. This classification is commonly used in botanical nomenclature to categorize and communicate information about hybrid plants.

intro: Serapias × dupuyana, also known as the Dupuyan serapias, is a rare, hybrid orchid species that is endemic to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Serapias × dupuyana include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
